journeys.title: Buyer-Seller Messaging
journeys.description: Buyers and sellers exchange messages in typed threads (inquiry, direct, offer, custom request) with realtime delivery, typing indicators, read receipts, online status, safety filtering, unread tracking, and archiving.

nodes.label: Open Messaging
nodes.notes: Entry point for all messaging flows. Accessible from the nav inbox badge or from a listing detail page CTA. Route is /messages — auth-guarded by proxy.ts.

nodes.label: Show Loading Skeleton
nodes.why: Renders 5 skeleton rows with role="status" and sr-only "Loading your messages" while useThreads() is pending. Each skeleton row has avatar, body, and timestamp placeholders.

nodes.label: Fetch Thread List
nodes.why: Returns all threads where user is a participant, joined with participant member details and my_unread_count. Optional ?type= filter accepts: inquiry, direct, offer, custom_request. Client fetches all threads then filters client-side by active tab.

nodes.label: Fetch Unread Count
nodes.why: Sum of unread_count across all active participant rows for this user. Drives the inbox badge in the navigation header. Query key: ['messaging', 'unread-count']. No polling — invalidated by useRealtimeUnreadCount on participant row changes.

nodes.label: Show Error State
nodes.why: ErrorState banner: "Something went wrong loading your messages" with a Retry button that calls refetch(). Shown when useThreads() isError is true.

nodes.label: Render Filter Tabs
nodes.why: Tabs component with five tabs: All, Inquiries, Offers, Custom Requests, Direct. Each tab shows its unread count badge (summed from my_unread_count across filtered threads). Switching tabs filters allThreads client-side by thread.type — no additional API call.

nodes.label: Render Thread List
nodes.why: ThreadList renders a <ul> of ThreadRow components. Each ThreadRow links to /messages/{thread_id} and shows: other participant avatar, name (bold if unread), TypeBadge (colored pill for thread type), last_message_preview truncated, relative timestamp (Just now / Xm ago / Xh ago / Yesterday / MMM D), and an unread dot indicator. When last_message_preview equals 'Sent a photo' (IMAGE_MESSAGE_PREVIEW constant), a HiCamera icon is prepended to the preview text.

nodes.label: Show Empty State
nodes.why: Empty state with chat icon, "No messages yet" heading, tab-contextual description (e.g. "You have no offers yet" on the Offers tab), and a "Browse listings" CTA that routes to /search. Shown when the filtered thread list has zero items.

nodes.label: Create Thread
nodes.why: Body: { type, participantIds, roles, listingId?, shopId? }. Returns 201 for new thread, 409 if a thread with the same participants and type already exists (client should redirect to the existing thread). type must be one of: inquiry, direct, offer, custom_request. listingId required for inquiry; shopId required for custom_request. Any authenticated member can create a direct thread with any other member — no prior transaction required. A bidirectional block check returns 403 if either member has blocked the other.

errorCases.condition: roles length does not match participantIds
A block relationship exists between the two members (either direction)
errorCases.result: Returns 403 — You cannot message this user
Returns 409 — client redirects to existing thread

nodes.label: Show Block Error
nodes.notes: On 403 from POST /api/messaging/threads: the block check prevents thread creation when either member has blocked the other. The generic error toast in MessageButton displays 'Something went wrong — Please try again.' Block enforcement is bidirectional — checked in the POST handler before calling createThreadServer.

nodes.label: Open New Thread
nodes.notes: On 201: navigate to the new thread view. On 409: redirect to the existing thread (the response body contains the existing ThreadWithParticipants). The client service createThread() returns the thread in both cases — callers should differentiate via HTTP status if needed.

nodes.label: Fetch Thread Details
nodes.why: Returns ThreadWithParticipants: thread row joined with participant member details (name, avatar, slug) and my_unread_count. Returns 404 if the thread does not exist or user is not a participant (participant check is in getThreadByIdServer).

errorCases.condition: Thread not found or user not a participant

nodes.label: Fetch Messages
nodes.why: Cursor-based pagination. Query params: ?cursor={messageId}&limit={1-100, default 50}. Returns { messages: MessageWithSender[], nextCursor: string | null }. Messages ordered newest-first. Non-participant request returns 403.

errorCases.condition: User is not a thread participant

nodes.label: Mark Thread Read
nodes.why: Resets unread_count to 0 and sets last_read_at to now on the user's message_thread_participants row. Called automatically on thread open. last_read_at is used by the other participant to determine the read receipt threshold.

errorCases.condition: User is not a thread participant

nodes.label: Subscribe to Messages
nodes.why: useRealtimeMessages(threadId, userId) opens a Supabase Realtime channel subscribed to INSERT events on the messages table filtered by thread_id. Incoming messages from other users are prepended to the first page of the useMessages infinite query cache immediately. Messages from the current user are skipped — they are already handled by the optimistic update in useSendMessage. After prepending, the query is also invalidated to hydrate full sender data on the next fetch.

nodes.label: Show Thread Skeleton
nodes.why: Rendered while useThread() or useMessages() isPending. Shows a skeleton header block and 6 alternating left/right bubble rows (3 left, 3 right) with avatar placeholders. Has role="status" aria-live="polite" and sr-only "Loading conversation". ComposeBar is rendered disabled during load.

nodes.label: Show Thread Error State
nodes.why: Rendered when useThread() or useMessages() isError is true. Shows ErrorState banner variant with message "Something went wrong loading this conversation" and a Retry button that calls refetchThread() and refetchMessages() together. ComposeBar is rendered disabled.

nodes.label: Render Collapsible Header
nodes.why: Thread context header above the message area. Renders thread-type-specific content: inquiry threads show participant avatar, name link (/member/{slug}), and a link to the listing ("About: Listing #{id}"); offer threads show an OfferBubble with accept/decline/counter actions if the current user is the seller and the offer is pending; direct and custom_request threads show participant avatar and name link. When collapsed, renders a slim bar showing TypeBadge + participant first name. Toggle is aria-expanded and aria-label aware.

nodes.label: Render Thread View
nodes.why: Full thread detail page at /messages/[thread_id]. Composed of: CollapsibleHeader (thread context), a scrollable message area with MessageThread (chat bubbles, system/nudge nodes, listing/offer nodes, date separators), and ComposeBar at the bottom. On initial data load, scrolls to the bottom of the message area. useMarkRead() fires on mount to clear the unread count.

nodes.label: Show Empty Thread State
nodes.why: Rendered when messages array has zero items after a successful load. Shows centered paragraph: "Send a message to get started". ComposeBar remains enabled so the user can send the first message.

nodes.label: Show Archived/Closed Banner
nodes.why: Rendered when thread.status is 'archived' or 'closed'. Shows an InlineBanner (info variant) above the ComposeBar with label "This conversation is archived" or "This conversation is closed". ComposeBar is rendered with disabled=true so the user cannot send new messages.

nodes.label: Load Older Messages
nodes.why: IntersectionObserver on a sentinel div pinned to the top of the message area. When the sentinel enters the viewport and hasNextPage is true, fetchNextPage() is called (useMessages infinite query). Scroll position is preserved after new pages append: previousScrollHeight is captured before fetch and the container.scrollTop is adjusted by the height diff after the new messages render.

nodes.label: Compose Message
nodes.why: Auto-expanding textarea at the bottom of the thread view. Grows up to 5 rows then scrolls. Enter submits the message; Shift+Enter inserts a newline. Send button is disabled when input is empty or a send is in-flight (aria-busy). Each keystroke calls onTyping() which triggers the Broadcast channel typing event (throttled to once per second). The + button opens an action menu with: 'Send Photo' (opens file picker for up to 4 images), 'Make an Offer' (inquiry threads only, buyer role only), and 'Share a Listing' (disabled — not yet built). Menu closes on outside click or Escape.

nodes.label: Broadcast Typing
nodes.why: useTypingIndicator(threadId, userId) opens a Supabase Realtime Broadcast channel named 'typing-{threadId}'. When the user types, startTyping() sends { userId, threadId } on the 'typing' event. The hook receives incoming events and sets isOtherTyping=true, then auto-clears it after 3 seconds of inactivity via a debounced timeout. The current user's own events are filtered out by comparing payload.userId to currentUserId.

nodes.label: Show Typing Indicator
nodes.why: Rendered below the message area when isOtherTyping is true. Shows the other participant's first name + animated three-dot indicator (e.g. 'Alex is typing...'). Uses role="status" aria-live="polite" for screen reader announcements. Auto-disappears after 3 seconds if no new typing event is received.

nodes.label: Send Message
nodes.why: Body: { content, type? }. type defaults to 'text'. Runs participant check, block check, and safety filter (block → 422, redact → stored filtered, nudge → nudge system message appended, pass → as-is). Returns 201 MessageWithSender.

errorCases.condition: User is not a thread participant

errorCases.result: Returns 403 — cannot send to this user

nodes.label: Apply Safety Filter
nodes.why: Processing order: block → redact → nudge → pass. Block: explicit/profane content → 422 rejected. Redact: PII (phone, email, credit card via Luhn) → replaced with [removed], original stored in original_content with is_filtered=true. Nudge: off-platform dealing or price negotiation → message passes but a nudge system message is inserted after it. Pass: no match → stored as-is.

nodes.label: Update Thread Preview
nodes.why: createMessageServer() updates last_message_at and last_message_preview on the message_threads row, and increments unread_count on all other participants' message_thread_participants rows.

nodes.label: Notify Recipient
nodes.why: Fire-and-forget: sent only when the recipient's unread_count transitions from 0 to 1 (i.e. this is their first unread message in the thread). Subject: 'New message from {senderName}'. Includes message preview (truncated at 200 chars) and a deep link to the thread. Template: src/features/email/templates/new-message.ts.

nodes.label: In-App Notification
nodes.why: Fire-and-forget: dispatched for text-type messages only via the send-message API route (type === 'text'). Fetches sender name from members table, truncates message content to 100 chars for the notification body, then calls dispatchNotification() for each non-sender participant. type: 'new_message', link: /messages/{thread_id}. Runs in a separate void async IIFE after the email notification block — never blocks the API response.

nodes.label: Message Appears
nodes.why: useSendMessage uses optimistic updates: the message is prepended to the first page of the infinite query cache immediately on mutate, before the server responds. On success: input clears, textarea height resets, and focus returns to the textarea. On error: the optimistic message is rolled back via the onError context snapshot. The mutation settles by invalidating both the messages query and the threads list query.

nodes.label: Show Nudge Message
nodes.why: When the safety filter returns nudge_off_platform or nudge_negotiation, the API inserts a second message with type='nudge' immediately after the user's message. MessageNode renders nudge-type messages as an InlineBanner (info variant) centered in the thread — not attributed to a sender. This guides the user toward platform-native flows (e.g. the offer system) without blocking their original message.

nodes.label: Show New Message Pill
nodes.why: When a new message arrives via Realtime and the user is scrolled more than 150px from the bottom of the message area, a "New message" pill button appears at the bottom of the message viewport. Clicking it smooth-scrolls to the bottom and dismisses the pill. If the user is already near the bottom, the view auto-scrolls instead and the pill is never shown.

nodes.label: Open Action Menu
nodes.why: User taps the + button in the ComposeBar. A popover menu opens with role='menu': 'Send Photo' (HiCamera icon), 'Make an Offer' (inquiry threads, buyer role only), and 'Share a Listing' (disabled). Clicking 'Send Photo' calls handlePhotoSelect(), closes the menu, and programmatically clicks the hidden file input. Menu closes on outside click or Escape.

nodes.label: Select Images from File Picker
nodes.why: Hidden <input type='file' accept='image/jpeg,image/png,image/webp,image/gif' multiple> opens the native file picker. On change, handleFileChange() validates: MIME type (JPEG/PNG/WebP/GIF only), file size (5MB max per file), and count (max 4 files — first 4 accepted, rest silently dropped with a count warning). Valid files are stored in selectedFiles state. Input value is reset so the same file can be re-selected.

nodes.label: Show Validation Error
nodes.why:
If any file fails MIME or size validation: imageError state is set and a

renders below the ImagePreviewStrip with the error text (e.g. 'Invalid file type. Accepted: JPEG, PNG, WebP, GIF' or 'One or more files exceed the 5MB limit'). No files are accepted. If count exceeds 4, first 4 are accepted and error reads 'Maximum 4 images per message. Only the first 4 were selected.'

nodes.label: Show Image Preview Strip
nodes.why: ImagePreviewStrip renders above the compose bar textarea when selectedFiles is non-empty. Shows a horizontal thumbnail list using URL.createObjectURL() for each file (revoked on unmount). Each thumbnail has an 'X' remove button. A count indicator shows '{n}/4'. A send button (HiPaperAirplane icon) triggers handleSendImages(). Strip returns null when no files are selected.

nodes.label: Tap Send in Preview Strip
nodes.why: User taps the send button in the ImagePreviewStrip. handleSendImages() calls sendImages(selectedFiles) via useSendImages mutation. The send button in the strip is disabled and aria-busy during the upload. The main textarea send button is also disabled while isUploadPending is true.

nodes.label: Prepend Optimistic Message
nodes.why: useSendImages onMutate: cancels in-flight messages queries, snapshots previous state, then prepends an optimistic MessageWithSender (type: 'image', content: 'Sent a photo', metadata: null) to the first page of the useMessages infinite query cache. The optimistic message has a random UUID and a placeholder sender with empty name fields. On error: cache is rolled back via the snapshot context.

nodes.label: Upload Images
nodes.why: multipart/form-data body: files[] (1–4 files). Auth check → participant check (403 if not participant) → block check (403 if recipient has blocked sender) → file count validation (max 4) → per-file MIME and size validation (422 on failure). For each file: scanImage() moderation → Sharp resize to 1200×1200 max (fit: inside, no upscale) → convert to WebP at 80% quality → upload to 'message-images' bucket at threads/{thread_id}/{message_id}/{uuid}.webp. On any upload failure, already-uploaded files are cleaned up. Calls createMessageServer() with type: 'image' and metadata: { images: ImageAttachment[] }. Returns 201 MessageWithSender.

errorCases.condition: User is not a thread participant

errorCases.result: Returns 403 — You cannot send messages to this user
Returns 400 — Too many files. Maximum is 4
Returns 422 — Invalid file type. Accepted: JPEG, PNG, WebP, GIF
Returns 422 — Image failed moderation check. Already-uploaded files in the batch are deleted from storage.
Returns 500 — Failed to upload image. Already-uploaded files in the batch are deleted.

nodes.label: Scan Image
nodes.why: scanImage(buffer) runs before each file is processed by Sharp. Currently a pass-through stub that always returns { passed: true } — will be wired to the real image scanner when issue #318 (platform-wide content moderation) lands. Returns { passed: boolean; reason?: string }.

nodes.label: Update Thread Preview
nodes.why: createMessageServer() sets last_message_preview to 'Sent a photo' (IMAGE_MESSAGE_PREVIEW) and updates last_message_at on the message_threads row. Increments unread_count on all other participants' message_thread_participants rows.

nodes.label: Notify Recipient by Email
nodes.why: Fire-and-forget: sent only when the recipient's unread_count transitions from 0 to 1. Uses the newMessage email template with messagePreview: 'Sent a photo'. Subject: 'New message from {senderName}'. Deep link to the thread. Checks notification_preferences.email.community_messages before sending. Silently catches all errors.

nodes.label: In-App Notification
nodes.why: Fire-and-forget: dispatched for each non-sender participant regardless of unread count. Calls dispatchNotification() with type: 'new_message', body: 'Sent a photo', link: /messages/{thread_id}. Runs in the same void async IIFE as the email notification — never blocks the API response.

nodes.label: Show Upload Error
nodes.why:
On useSendImages onError: the optimistic message is rolled back from the cache, selectedFiles state is preserved (user can retry), and the onError callback sets imageError state. The

renders the error message below the preview strip.

nodes.label: Render Image Message
nodes.why: MessageThread renders type='image' messages via ImageNode. The optimistic message has metadata: null so renders a loading placeholder; on query invalidation and refetch, the real metadata ({ images: ImageAttachment[] }) hydrates and ImageNode renders the grid. Layout adapts to image count: 1 image = full-width; 2 images = side-by-side; 3–4 images = 2×2 grid. Each image is a <button> triggering the lightbox at that index. Uses next/image with fill and object-fit: cover.

nodes.label: Open Image Lightbox
nodes.why: Tapping any image in an ImageNode calls onImageClick(index), which sets lightboxOpen=true and lightboxInitialIndex to the tapped index on MessageThread. ImageLightbox renders via ReactDOM.createPortal to #modal-root. Shows the tapped image full-screen (next/image fill, object-fit: contain). Multi-image messages show Prev/Next chevron buttons and a '{current} / {total}' counter. Escape key closes. Arrow keys navigate. Focus trap active — Tab cycles within the lightbox. Body scroll locked while open. On close, focus returns to the triggering image button.

nodes.label: Receive Message Event
nodes.why: Supabase Realtime INSERT event fires on the messages table for the open thread_id channel. The hook prepends the incoming message to the first page of the useMessages cache. Sender data from the Realtime payload has placeholder name fields; a follow-up query invalidation hydrates the full sender profile on the next refetch. Deduplication: if the message ID already exists in the first page (from an optimistic insert by the current user), it is skipped.

nodes.label: Scroll or Show Pill
nodes.why: On latestMessageId change: if the user is within 150px of the bottom (isNearBottom()), the thread area smooth-scrolls to the bottom. If the user is scrolled further up, the 'New message' pill appears instead to avoid interrupting their scroll position.

nodes.label: Show Read Receipt
nodes.why: Below the last sent text message (identified by lastSentMessageIndex), a 'Read' label appears when otherParticipantLastReadAt is set and is newer than that message's created_at. The last_read_at value comes from the ThreadWithParticipants data returned by useThread() — it is set by PATCH /api/messaging/threads/{id}/read when the other participant opens the thread.

nodes.label: Send Heartbeat
nodes.why: useOnlineStatus(enabled) fires PATCH /api/members/heartbeat on mount and then every 60 seconds. The request sets last_seen_at = now() on the members row for the authenticated user. Best-effort — failures are silently swallowed. Enabled is false for unauthenticated users.

nodes.label: Show Online Indicator
nodes.notes: isOnline(lastSeenAt) utility returns true if last_seen_at is within the last 5 minutes. A green dot indicator on participant avatars (thread header, inbox rows) is planned but not yet wired into the UI components. The heartbeat and isOnline() logic are built; the rendering layer is still outstanding.

nodes.label: Archive Thread
nodes.why: Sets thread_status = 'archived' on the message_threads row. Archived threads are excluded from the default inbox view. Returns { success: true }. Non-participant request returns 403.

errorCases.condition: User is not a thread participant

nodes.label: Thread Removed
nodes.notes: On success: optimistic removal from the active inbox list, toast 'Conversation archived'. Thread should reappear if the user opens an Archived filter tab. useArchiveThread mutation should invalidate ['messaging', 'threads'].

nodes.label: Open Offer Sheet
nodes.why: Seller clicks 'Make Offer' in the ComposeBar (available for inquiry threads when currentUserRole is 'buyer'). Sets offerSheetMode='create' and opens the OfferSheet bottom drawer. The sheet shows listing title, price, the 70% minimum floor, and an amount input prefilled at 80% of listing price.

nodes.label: Validate Offer Amount
nodes.why: Client-side validation using validateOfferAmount(amountCents, listingPriceCents) before submitting. Minimum is 70% of listing price (OFFER_MIN_PERCENTAGE = 0.70). Shows inline error if below floor.

nodes.label: Submit Offer
nodes.why: createOfferServer() validates: listing is active, buyer is not the seller, amount >= 70% of price, seller is valid; expires stale offers; creates offer thread + offer row in one operation. Returns the created Offer.

nodes.label: Notify Seller
nodes.why: Fire-and-forget email to seller after offer creation. Subject: 'New offer on {listingTitle}'. Includes offer amount and a CTA linking to the offer thread. Template: src/features/email/templates/offer-notification.ts (type: 'received').

nodes.label: Offer Thread Opens
nodes.why: createOfferServer() creates a thread of type='offer' as part of the offer creation. handleOfferCreated() receives the new offer and navigates to /messages/{newOffer.thread_id}, opening the offer thread where the offer appears as an offer_node message rendered by OfferBubble.

nodes.label: Seller Reviews Offer
nodes.why: CollapsibleHeader shows an OfferBubble for offer-type threads when offer data is loaded. Accept and Decline action buttons are rendered to the seller when offer.status is 'pending' (isSeller check via participant role). Counter-offer opens the OfferSheet in 'counter' mode. Accept/decline/counter trigger useOfferActions mutations with optimistic status updates. OfferBubble handles all 5 statuses: pending, accepted, declined, countered, expired.

nodes.label: Accept Offer
nodes.why: Seller-only, pending-only. Calls acceptOfferServer() which sets offer status to 'accepted' and inserts a system message into the thread. Returns 403 if caller is not the seller; 409 if offer is no longer pending.

nodes.label: Notify Buyer Accepted
nodes.why: Fire-and-forget email to buyer after seller accepts the offer. Subject: 'Your offer on {listingTitle} was accepted!'. CTA label: 'Continue to Checkout'. Template: src/features/email/templates/offer-notification.ts (type: 'accepted').

nodes.label: Decline Offer
nodes.why: Seller-only, pending-only. Calls declineOfferServer() which sets offer status to 'declined' and inserts a system message into the thread. Returns 403 if caller is not the seller; 409 if offer is no longer pending.

nodes.label: Notify Buyer Declined
nodes.why: Fire-and-forget email to buyer after seller declines the offer. Subject: 'Your offer on {listingTitle} was declined'. CTA label: 'View Thread'. Template: src/features/email/templates/offer-notification.ts (type: 'declined').

nodes.label: Counter Offer
nodes.why: Seller-only. Calls counterOfferServer() which marks the original offer as 'countered' and creates a new offer row with buyer/seller roles swapped and parent_offer_id set. Returns 403 if caller is not the seller; 409 if offer is no longer pending; 400 if amountCents is missing or below floor.

errorCases.condition: Counter amount below 70% floor or zero

nodes.label: Notify Buyer Countered
nodes.why: Fire-and-forget email to the original offerer after a counter is made. Subject: 'Counter offer on {listingTitle}'. CTA label: 'View Counter Offer'. Template: src/features/email/templates/offer-notification.ts (type: 'countered').

nodes.label: Expire Stale Offers
nodes.notes: Vercel cron at GET /api/cron/offers-expiry runs daily (Hobby plan). expirePendingOffersServer() expires pending offers after 24h and accepted offers after 4h checkout window. Inserts system message 'This offer has expired.' into each offer thread. On Pro plan, increase frequency to every 15 minutes.

nodes.label: Click Block User
nodes.notes: Block entry point from a messaging thread — e.g., kebab menu in the thread header with a 'Block this user' option. Client calls blockMember(memberId) from src/features/messaging/services/blocks.ts. Thread UI is Phase 3; this step is planned alongside it.

nodes.label: Create Block Record
nodes.why: Body: { memberId }. Auth check via Supabase session. Self-block guard (400). Calls blockMemberServer() which inserts into member_blocks. Returns 201 MemberBlock. Returns 409 if already blocked.

errorCases.condition: memberId is missing from request body

nodes.label: Show Block Feedback
nodes.notes: On 201 or 409: show success toast (e.g., 'Blocked — You can no longer receive messages from this user.'). Thread should archive or close. The blocked user will receive 403 on any further message sends to this thread. Invalidate ['blocks', userId] so the account settings blocked-members list stays in sync. See member-block-management.json for the full block management flow.

nodes.label: Show Block Error
nodes.notes: On 400 or 500: show error toast (e.g., 'Could not block this user. Please try again.'). Thread remains open.

nodes.label: Fetch Unread Count
nodes.why: Sums unread_count across all active participant rows. useUnreadCount() has no polling interval — the count stays fresh via useRealtimeUnreadCount, which invalidates this query key on any UPDATE to the user's message_thread_participants rows. Query key: ['messages', 'unread-count'].

nodes.label: Subscribe to Unread Changes
nodes.why: useRealtimeUnreadCount(userId) subscribes to UPDATE events on message_thread_participants filtered by member_id. On any change, it invalidates both ['messages', 'unread-count'] and ['messages', 'threads'] query keys, triggering a refetch of the badge count and thread list. This replaces the previous polling-based approach.

nodes.label: Render Unread Badge
nodes.notes: Nav inbox icon badge showing total unread count. Should be driven by useUnreadCount() hook. Badge should be hidden at 0 and cap display at 99+. Needs to invalidate when markThreadRead succeeds.

journeys.title: Cart (Guest + Authenticated)
journeys.description: Cart system with dual storage: localStorage for guests, DB-backed with price snapshots for authenticated users. Includes merge on login, seller grouping, pre-checkout validation, and block enforcement.

nodes.label: Validate Cart State
nodes.why: Client-side validation: own listing, already in cart, cart full

nodes.label: Add to Cart (DB)
nodes.why: Send listingId + priceCents. Server checks: listing exists + active, block check (isBlockedByServer), not own listing, no duplicate, cart cap.

nodes.label: Save Price Snapshot
nodes.why: Price snapshot saved as price_at_add

nodes.label: Update Cache
nodes.why: Optimistic cache update

nodes.label: Save to localStorage
nodes.why: Add item to localStorage cart (listingId + priceCents snapshot). No API calls.

nodes.label: Update Cart Badge
nodes.why: CartIcon badge count updates via Zustand store

nodes.label: Show Added Toast
nodes.why: Toast: 'Added to cart'

nodes.label: Check Auth State
nodes.why: Determine storage strategy: DB-backed for authenticated users, localStorage for guests

nodes.label: Fetch Cart Items (DB)
nodes.why: Returns cart items joined with listing data

nodes.label: Group by Seller
nodes.why: Items grouped by seller for display

nodes.label: Show Item States
nodes.why: States: valid, price changed (old vs new), removed/sold (warning)

nodes.label: Render Cart Items
nodes.why: Cart items with remove/clear actions. Checkout button disabled in MVP.

nodes.label: Show Cart Actions
nodes.why: Actions: remove item, clear all, refresh expiry

nodes.label: Detect Auth Change
nodes.why: useCartMerge() detects auth state change. Background hook — user sees nothing.

nodes.label: Read Guest Cart
nodes.why: Read localStorage cart items

nodes.label: Merge Cart to DB
nodes.why: Server validates every guest item: listing exists, not sold, not own listing, not already in DB cart.

nodes.label: Skip Invalid Items
nodes.why: Invalid/duplicate/own-listing items silently dropped. No errors surfaced to user.

nodes.label: Insert Valid Items
nodes.why: Insert valid items into cart table

nodes.label: Clear Guest Cart
nodes.why: Clear localStorage guest cart. No toast, no redirect. Cart page now shows DB-backed items.

nodes.label: Validate Cart
nodes.why: Validates all cart items. Batch block check: single IN query on member_blocks for all unique seller IDs. Blocked items moved to removed[] with reason: 'blocked'.

nodes.label: Handle Result
nodes.why: Handle validation result

nodes.label: Cart Ready
nodes.why: Items render in seller-grouped sections. Checkout button enabled.

nodes.label: Show Price Change
nodes.why: Price change UI not yet implemented. Validation detects changes but cart page does not display them.

nodes.label: Show Removed Banner
nodes.why: Stale banner: "{count} items were removed" with per-item reasons (Sold, Deactivated, Unavailable). Dismiss button. Items auto-removed from cart.

journeys.description: Identity system overview: member/shop context switching via Zustand store, X-Nessi-Context header on every request with server-side validation, localStorage persistence, page load rehydration, and edge case handling.

nodes.why: User logs in — default: { type: 'member' }

nodes.label: Check Shop Membership
nodes.why: Check if user belongs to shops

nodes.label: Show Context Switcher

nodes.label: Switch to Member
nodes.why: switchToMember() — Zustand: { type: 'member' }, X-Nessi-Context: member

nodes.label: Switch to Shop

nodes.label: Persist Context

nodes.label: Member Dashboard

nodes.label: Shop Dashboard
nodes.why: Shop dashboard KPIs

nodes.why: Zustand rehydrates from localStorage

nodes.label: Restore Member

nodes.label: Restore Shop

nodes.why: First API call validates shop membership

nodes.label: Redirect Home

journeys.title: Blocked Members
journeys.description: View and manage blocked members — authenticated users can see who they have blocked and unblock them from the account settings page. Block enforcement prevents blocked users from viewing the blocker's profiles, shops, listings, and purchasing their items.

nodes.label: Trigger Block Action
nodes.notes: The block action client service (blockMember) is built, but the UI entry point is planned. Expected surfaces: message thread header (block this user), member profile page (block button). Calls blockMember(memberId) which posts to /api/members/block.

nodes.label: Create Block Record
nodes.why: Body: { memberId }. Auth check via Supabase session. Validates memberId is present and is not the requesting user (self-block guard). Calls blockMemberServer(user.id, memberId). Returns 201 MemberBlock on success.

errorCases.condition: memberId is missing from request body
memberId equals the authenticated user's own ID

nodes.label: Insert Block Row
nodes.why: INSERT into member_blocks (blocker_id, blocked_id). DB-level UNIQUE (blocker_id, blocked_id) constraint and CHECK (blocker_id != blocked_id) constraint enforce integrity. RLS policy member_blocks_insert_own enforces blocker_id = auth.uid().

nodes.label: Show Block Confirmation
nodes.notes: On 201: show success toast confirming the block (e.g., 'Blocked — You blocked {name}.'). On 409: silently succeed or show 'Already blocked'. UI should update immediately — e.g., disable the Message button in a thread header, or replace the block button with an unblock option. Cache for ['blocks', userId] should be invalidated so the blocked members list reflects the new entry.

nodes.label: Show Block Error
nodes.notes: On 400 or 500: show error toast (e.g., 'Could not block this member. Please try again.'). No cache changes — optimistic update should not be applied for the block action given its low frequency.

nodes.label: Fetch Blocked Members
nodes.why: Auth check via Supabase session. Returns all member_blocks rows where blocker_id = current user, joined with members table to resolve name, avatar_url, and slug. Ordered by created_at DESC.

nodes.label: Resolve Member Details
nodes.why: Server service joins member_blocks with members table in two queries: first fetches block rows, then fetches member rows by blocked_id array. Builds a map of id → {name, avatar_url, slug} to avoid N+1 queries.

nodes.label: Show Loading Skeleton
nodes.why: Four skeleton cards rendered while useBlockedMembers query is in-flight. role=status with aria-label for screen readers.

nodes.label: Click Unblock
nodes.why: Unblock button has aria-label='Unblock {name}'. Triggers useUnblockMember mutation. Button shows loading spinner while isPending.

nodes.label: Remove from Cache
nodes.why: onMutate: cancels in-flight ['blocks', userId] queries, snapshots previous data, filters the unblocked member out of cache immediately. Rolled back on error.

nodes.label: Delete Block Record
nodes.why: Used by the account-settings unblock UI. blocked_id passed as query param (?blocked_id={uuid}). Auth check via session. Deletes from member_blocks where blocker_id = current user AND blocked_id = param. Returns 404 if no matching row found. Note: a second unblock route exists at DELETE /api/members/block/[member_id] — used from messaging surfaces via blockMember/unblockMember in src/features/messaging/services/blocks.ts.

errorCases.condition: blocked_id param is missing or empty
Block record not found (already unblocked or wrong user)

nodes.label: Delete Row
nodes.why: DELETE from member_blocks WHERE blocker_id = blockerId AND blocked_id = blockedId. RLS policy enforces blocker_id = auth.uid(). Unique constraint on (blocker_id, blocked_id) means at most one row deleted.

nodes.label: Invalidate Query Cache
nodes.why: onSettled always invalidates ['blocks', userId] to sync the server state after optimistic update.

nodes.label: Show Success Toast
nodes.why: Toast: title 'Unblocked', description 'You unblocked {name}.' type=success.

nodes.label: Show Error Toast
nodes.why: Toast: title 'Something went wrong', description 'Could not unblock this member. Please try again.' type=error. Optimistic cache removal is rolled back via onError snapshot restore.

nodes.label: Click Member Profile
nodes.why: Both avatar and name are wrapped in AppLink. Href resolves to /member/{slug} if slug exists, otherwise /member/{blocked_id}.

nodes.label: Navigate to Profile
nodes.why: Standard member profile page. Block relationship is not surfaced here yet — a 'Blocked' indicator on the profile page is a planned improvement.

nodes.label: Show Blocked Badge
nodes.notes: When viewing a member profile from the blocked list (or any profile of a member you have blocked), show a 'Blocked' indicator. This would let users confirm the block is active and access the unblock action without navigating to account settings.

nodes.label: Fetch Member by Slug
nodes.why: Server component calls getMemberBySlugServer(slug). If member not found, notFound() is called.

nodes.label: Check Block Status
nodes.why: Calls isBlockedByServer(currentUserId, member.id). Uses server client with RLS policy member_blocks_select_blocked (blocked_id = auth.uid()). Short-circuits to false for null viewer or self-view.

nodes.label: Show Not Found Page
nodes.why: If blocked, notFound() is called — identical to a non-existent member. No information leakage about the block.

nodes.label: Fetch Shop by Slug
nodes.why: Server component calls getShopBySlugServer(slug). Returns shop with owner_id. If shop not found, notFound() is called.

nodes.label: Check Block Status
nodes.why: Calls isBlockedByServer(currentUserId, shop.owner_id). Block is checked against the shop owner, not the shop entity itself.

nodes.label: Is viewer blocked by shop owner?
Show Not Found Page
nodes.why: If blocked, notFound() is called — identical to a non-existent shop.

nodes.label: Fetch Listing with Seller
nodes.why: Server component calls getListingWithSellerServer(id). Returns listing with seller_id. If listing not found or inactive, notFound() is called.

nodes.label: Check Block Status
nodes.why: Calls isBlockedByServer(currentUserId, listing.seller_id). Block is checked against the listing's seller.

nodes.label: Show Not Found Page
nodes.why: If blocked, notFound() is called — identical to a non-existent listing.

nodes.label: Add to Cart (Blocked Seller)
nodes.why: addToCartServer checks isBlockedByServer(userId, listing.seller_id) after fetching listing. If blocked, throws 'Seller has blocked you'.

nodes.label: Validate Cart (Blocked Items)
nodes.why: validateCartServer performs a single batch IN query on member_blocks for all unique seller IDs. Items from blocked sellers are moved to the 'removed' array with reason: 'blocked'.

nodes.label: Show Removed Items Banner
nodes.why: Cart page stale items banner shows blocked items as removed. The 'blocked' reason is included in the CartValidationResult removed-item reason union type.

journeys.title: Follow / Unfollow
journeys.description: Follow or unfollow members and shops to get updates on their activity.

nodes.label: View Profile Page
nodes.why: User views a member profile or shop page with a FollowButton and follower count. Button hidden when viewing own profile/shop.

nodes.label: Tap Follow Button
nodes.why: FollowButton checks auth (shows sign-in toast if unauthenticated). useFollowToggle fires mutation with isCurrentlyFollowing=false, optimistically sets status to following and increments count cache.

nodes.label: Create Follow
nodes.why: Validates target_type (member|shop), target_id, checks auth, calls createFollowServer. Returns 201 on success.

nodes.label: Insert Follow Row
nodes.why: INSERT into follows table. update_follower_count() trigger increments target's follower_count column.

nodes.label: Show Following State
nodes.why: Button updates to 'Following' state (filled primary), follower count increments. Toast: 'Following — You are now following {name}.' onSettled invalidates status, count, and following queries.

nodes.label: View Followed Entity
nodes.why: User views a member or shop they already follow. FollowButton shows 'Following' state. On hover/focus, changes to 'Unfollow' with destructive styling.

nodes.label: Tap Following Button
nodes.why: useFollowToggle fires mutation with isCurrentlyFollowing=true, optimistically sets status to not-following and decrements count cache. Toast: 'Unfollowed — You unfollowed {name}.'

nodes.label: Delete Follow
nodes.why: Reads target_type and target_id from query params (not body). Calls deleteFollowServer, returns 200 on success.

nodes.label: Delete Follow Row
nodes.why: DELETE from follows table. update_follower_count() trigger decrements target's follower_count (clamped to 0 via GREATEST).

nodes.label: Show Follow State
nodes.why: Button reverts to 'Follow' state (outline primary), follower count decrements. onSettled invalidates status, count, and following queries.

nodes.label: Navigate to Profile
nodes.why: FollowButton mounts on profile page. useFollowStatus hook fires query with targetType and targetId.

nodes.label: Fetch Follow Status
nodes.why: Returns { is_following: boolean } via getFollowStatusServer using maybeSingle() lookup

nodes.label: Render Button State
nodes.why: FollowButton renders as 'Follow' (outline) or 'Following' (filled primary) based on is_following response. Follower count badge also displayed.

nodes.label: Open Following Page
nodes.why: User navigates to /dashboard/following. Page is protected by proxy.ts. useFollowing() hook fires query on mount to fetch all followed entities.

nodes.label: Fetch Following List
nodes.why: Returns FollowingItem[] with name and avatar_url resolved via batch queries on members and shops tables. Optional target_type filter.

nodes.label: Render Following List
nodes.why: Renders FollowingCard for each item — avatar, linked name, type pill, relative date, Unfollow button. Each card manages its own useFollowToggle mutation with toast feedback. Empty state shows heart icon + 'You are not following anyone yet' + Browse CTA.

nodes.label: Show Empty State
nodes.why: Heart icon. Heading: "You are not following anyone yet". CTA: "Browse listings" link.

nodes.label: Load Homepage
nodes.why: FollowedSellersStrip mounts on the homepage (src/app/(frontend)/page.tsx). useFollowedSellerListings fires query when isAuthenticated && !authLoading. Strip is completely absent for guests — no empty state, no skeleton.

nodes.label: Fetch Followed Listings
nodes.why: Queries follows table for followed member/shop IDs, then fetches up to 20 recent active listings ordered by published_at descending. Returns { listings: ListingWithPhotos[] }. Returns { listings: [] } (not 404) when user has no follows.

errorCases.result: Failed to fetch followed seller listings

nodes.label: Render Listings Strip
nodes.why: ListingScrollStrip renders horizontally scrollable listing cards under the title 'From Sellers You Follow'. Component returns null when listings.length === 0 and isLoading is false — strip is invisible when the user follows no one or all followed sellers have no active listings.

nodes.label: Hide Strip Section
nodes.why: Strip section not rendered. No visible UI when user follows no one.

journeys.title: Watchlist & Price Alerts
journeys.description: Authenticated members watch listings via a heart icon toggle, view their watchlist page, and receive price drop email alerts when sellers reduce prices.

nodes.label: Click Heart Icon
nodes.why: WatchButton renders over listing images on ListingCard and on the detail page gallery. FaRegHeart (unwatched) / FaHeart (watched). Minimum 44x44px tap target.

nodes.label: Check Auth State
nodes.why: useAuth() — if !isAuthenticated, push to {pathname}?login=true to open the login modal. No toast shown — the modal open is the signal.

nodes.label: Optimistic UI Update
nodes.why: Cancels in-flight ['watchlist', 'status', listingId] queries, snapshots cache, and immediately flips is_watching in the cache before the API call resolves.

nodes.label: Watch Listing
nodes.why: Body: { listing_id }. Calls addWatcherServer(userId, listingId). On 23505 unique violation, returns the existing row rather than erroring.

errorCases.result: Returns 409 — client treats as success, no rollback

nodes.label: Unwatch Listing
nodes.why: Query param: ?listing_id=... Calls removeWatcherServer(userId, listingId). Returns 404 if watcher row not found — client treats as success.

errorCases.result: Returns 404 — client treats as success, no rollback

nodes.label: Update Watcher Count
nodes.why: AFTER INSERT OR DELETE trigger on watchers table. update_watcher_count() increments or decrements listings.watcher_count. Decrement is clamped to 0 via GREATEST(0, ...).

nodes.label: Show Result Toast
nodes.why: Watch success: 'Added to Watchlist — We\'ll tell you if the price drops.' Unwatch success: 'Removed from Watchlist — You will no longer receive alerts.' Error: 'Something went wrong — Please try again.'

nodes.label: Invalidate Query Cache
nodes.why: On settle (success or error): invalidates ['watchlist', 'status', listingId] and the root ['watchlist'] key, refreshing the watchlist page if open.

nodes.label: Fetch Watch Status
nodes.why: getWatchStatusServer(userId, listingId) — maybeSingle() lookup returns { is_watching: boolean }. Query key: ['watchlist', 'status', listingId]. Disabled when listingId is falsy.

errorCases.result: Returns 401 — WatchButton renders in unauthenticated state
Returns 500 — button defaults to unwatched state

nodes.label: Render Heart State
nodes.why: Defaults to is_watching: false while loading. aria-pressed reflects watch state. aria-busy true while mutation pending.

nodes.label: Route Guard
nodes.why: proxy.ts guards /watchlist — unauthenticated users are redirected to /. See route-protection.json.

nodes.label: Open Watchlist Page
nodes.why: Accessible via Watchlist link in the user dropdown nav (HiOutlineHeart icon).

nodes.label: Fetch Watched Listings
nodes.why: getWatchedListingsServer(userId) — joins watchers → listings → listing_photos. Filters soft-deleted listings. Ordered by watched_at DESC. Query key: ['watchlist'].

errorCases.result: Returns 500 — page shows error state with retry prompt

nodes.label: Render Listing Grid
nodes.why: Renders ListingCard for each WatchedListing. Sold listings show a 'Sold' overlay badge. Skeleton shown while loading (count: 8).

nodes.label: Show Empty State
nodes.why: Heart icon. "Your watchlist is empty". "Watch listings to get notified when prices drop." CTA: "Browse listings" → /listings

nodes.label: Detect Price Drop
nodes.why: AFTER UPDATE OF price_cents trigger on listings. detect_price_drop() fires when NEW.price_cents < OLD.price_cents — inserts a price_drop_notifications row with listing_id, old_price_cents, new_price_cents.

nodes.label: Cron Job Fires
nodes.why: Vercel cron schedule: */15 * * * * (every 15 minutes). Calls GET /api/cron/price-drops with Authorization: Bearer {CRON_SECRET}.

nodes.label: Fetch Notifications
nodes.why: Fetches up to 100 unprocessed rows (processed=false) from price_drop_notifications, ordered by created_at ASC (oldest first). Secured by CRON_SECRET bearer token.

errorCases.condition: Authorization header missing or incorrect CRON_SECRET

nodes.label: Resolve Eligible Watchers
nodes.why: For each notification: queries watchers WHERE listing_id matches AND (last_notified_price_cents IS NULL OR last_notified_price_cents > new_price_cents). Skips watchers already notified at or below the new price.

nodes.label: Look Up Watcher Email
nodes.why: auth.admin.getUserById(watcher.user_id) via admin client. If userError or no email, watcher is silently skipped.

nodes.label: Send Price Drop Email
nodes.why: Subject: '{listingTitle} just dropped in price!' Body: title, old price (strikethrough), new price, CTA button to /listing/{listingId}, fallback plain-text URL. listingTitle is HTML-escaped. Uses emailLayout() branded shell.

nodes.label: Record Notified Price
nodes.why: Updates watchers.last_notified_price_cents = new_price_cents for this watcher. Prevents duplicate notifications on successive smaller drops.

nodes.label: Mark Notification Done
nodes.why: Sets price_drop_notifications.processed = true after all watcher emails for that notification have been attempted. Returns { processed: N, emails_sent: N }.

nodes.label: Remove Watch on Sold
nodes.notes: When a listing transitions to status=sold, watchers rows should be deleted automatically (or watcher_count zeroed). Currently sold listings remain on the watchlist page with a Sold overlay but the user must manually unwatch. A DB trigger on listing status change to sold could clean up watchers rows, or the PATCH /api/listings/[id]/status handler could call removeWatchersForListing.

nodes.label: Unwatch in Email
nodes.notes: Price drop email has no unsubscribe or unwatch link. Adding a one-click unwatch token URL (e.g., /api/watchlist/unwatch?token=...) would reduce friction and follow email best practices. Token would encode listing_id + user_id and be validated server-side without requiring login.

nodes.label: Open Quick Edit Price
nodes.why: Seller clicks Quick Edit Price from the dashboard listing row actions menu.

nodes.label: Show Watcher Count
nodes.why: listings.watcher_count is denormalized and available on the listing object — no extra API call needed. Displayed as demand signal (e.g., 'X people are watching this listing').

nodes.label: Watcher Count on Row
nodes.notes: watcher_count is only shown inside QuickEditPrice. Surfacing it inline on the ListingRow (e.g., a small heart + count badge) would give sellers a at-a-glance demand signal without opening the modal.

journeys.title: Notification Center
journeys.description: Bell icon in navbar with unread count badge, activity feed dropdown with notification list, mark-as-read, realtime delivery via Supabase Realtime, and browser push notifications.

nodes.label: Show Bell Icon
nodes.why: NotificationBell renders in the navbar for all authenticated users. Displays a HiOutlineBell icon. When unread count > 0, a badge overlays the icon showing the count (capped at '9+' for counts above 9). aria-label is contextual: 'Notifications, N unread' or 'Notifications'. aria-expanded and aria-haspopup="dialog" are set on the toggle button.

nodes.label: Open Notification Panel
nodes.why: Clicking the bell toggles isOpen state. NotificationPanel is dynamically imported (ssr: false) and rendered when isOpen is true. Clicking outside the container (mousedown on document) closes the panel. Escape key also closes the panel via keydown handler inside NotificationPanel.

nodes.label: Fetch Notifications
nodes.why: Returns paginated notifications for the authenticated user. Query params: ?limit={1-100, default 20}&offset={default 0}. Ordered newest-first. Response: { notifications: Notification[], total: number }.

nodes.label: Show Loading Skeleton
nodes.why: While useNotifications() isPending, renders 4 skeleton rows. Each row has a circle placeholder (icon) and two line placeholders (title + body). Has no explicit aria-live but the panel itself has role="dialog".

nodes.label: Show Empty State
nodes.why: When notifications array is empty after a successful load, shows a HiCheckCircle icon and the text "You're all caught up!". No action buttons are shown.

nodes.label: Render Notification List
nodes.why: Maps over the notifications array and renders a NotificationItem for each. Header shows 'Notifications' title, a close button (X), and a 'Mark all as read' button that only appears when hasUnread is true. The panel has role="dialog" and aria-label="Notifications". A focus trap handles Tab/Shift+Tab cycling within the panel.

nodes.label: Click Notification
nodes.why: NotificationItem is a button. On click: if is_read is false, markAsRead(notification.id) is called. Then if notification.link is set, router.push(link) navigates to the target. The item renders: a type-specific colored icon circle, title (bold when unread), body text, relative timestamp (Just now / Xm / Xh / Xd / MMM D), and an unread dot indicator.

nodes.label: Optimistic Mark Read
nodes.why: onMutate: cancels in-flight queries for ['notifications'] and ['notifications', 'unread-count']. Snapshots previous cache state. Updates cache: sets is_read=true on the matched notification, decrements unread count by 1 (floor 0). If the API call fails, onError restores both snapshots.

nodes.label: Mark Notification Read
nodes.why: Sets is_read=true on the notification row. Ownership enforced via .eq('user_id', userId) — a notification not owned by the requesting user returns 404 (treated as not found).

errorCases.condition: Notification not found or not owned by user

nodes.label: Navigate to Target
nodes.why: After markAsRead fires (regardless of in-flight state), if notification.link is set, router.push(link) navigates the user. For new_message and offer notifications, link is /messages/{thread_id}. For order and review notifications, link is /dashboard/orders/{id} or /dashboard/reviews.

nodes.label: Mark All Read
nodes.why: Bulk updates all unread notifications for the authenticated user: sets is_read=true where user_id matches and is_read=false. Returns { success: true }.

nodes.label: Optimistic Clear All
nodes.why: onMutate: cancels in-flight queries, snapshots cache. Optimistically sets is_read=true on every notification in the list and sets unread count to 0. Button is disabled while isPending. On error, both snapshots are restored. onSettled invalidates both ['notifications'] and ['notifications', 'unread-count'] queries.

nodes.label: Panel Refreshed
nodes.why: After mutation settles, the 'Mark all as read' button disappears (hasUnread becomes false). All notification items lose their unread dot and bold title styling. The badge on the bell icon drops to 0 and disappears.

nodes.label: Receive Realtime Event
nodes.why: useRealtimeNotifications(userId) subscribes to INSERT events on the notifications table filtered by user_id via Supabase Realtime. On any new row, it invalidates both ['notifications'] and ['notifications', 'unread-count'] query keys, which triggers a refetch of the notification list and badge count. Replaces the previous 30-second polling interval on useUnreadNotificationCount.

nodes.label: Update Bell Badge
nodes.why: useUnreadNotificationCount() drives the badge count displayed on the bell. No polling — count stays fresh via the Realtime invalidation from useRealtimeNotifications. Badge shows numeric count capped at '9+'. Badge is hidden entirely when count is 0. aria-label on the bell button updates to include the count when unread > 0.

nodes.label: Fire Browser Notification
nodes.why: Inside the Realtime onPayload handler, showBrowserNotification(title, body) is called if the notification row has a title. Uses the Web Notifications API: checks Notification.permission === 'granted' before calling new Notification(title, { body }). Silently no-ops if the API is unavailable (SSR guard) or permission is not granted. Browser permission must be requested separately via requestNotificationPermission().

nodes.label: Request Notification Permission
nodes.notes: requestNotificationPermission() is implemented but not yet called from the UI. A permission prompt should be surfaced at an appropriate moment (e.g., first time the user opens the notification panel, or after they receive their first notification) to improve browser notification opt-in rates. Should only prompt once and store the result.

nodes.label: Fire Dispatch
nodes.why: dispatchNotification({ userId, type, title, body?, data?, link? }) is a fire-and-forget wrapper. Dynamically imports createNotificationServer to avoid loading the module on every request. Calls void on the async IIFE — never blocks the parent API response. Silently catches and console.errors all failures so notifications never break the triggering operation.

nodes.label: Create Notification Row
nodes.why: createNotificationServer() inserts a row into the notifications table using the admin client (bypasses RLS, required for cross-user notification creation). After insert, counts all notifications for the user. If count > 100, fetches the oldest (count - 100) rows and deletes them — enforcing the 100-notification cap per user. The INSERT event on the notifications table triggers the Supabase Realtime subscription in useRealtimeNotifications on the recipient's client.

errorCases.result: Throws — caught by dispatchNotification wrapper, silently logged
Throws — caught by dispatchNotification wrapper, silently logged
Throws — caught by dispatchNotification wrapper, silently logged

nodes.label: Notification Appears
nodes.why: The new notification row INSERT triggers the Realtime subscription in useRealtimeNotifications. Both ['notifications'] and ['notifications', 'unread-count'] are invalidated, causing the badge count to increment and the notification list to include the new item the next time the panel is opened. If the user already has the panel open, useNotifications() refetches and the new item appears in the list.

nodes.label: Close Notification Panel
nodes.why: Panel closes via: (1) clicking the X close button in the panel header, (2) pressing Escape (handled by keydown listener in NotificationPanel), or (3) clicking outside the container div (mousedown on document, checked via containerRef.current.contains). Sets isOpen to false, unmounting NotificationPanel.
